evals.prompt: We did a migration and I'm worried we broke some pages that used to get traffic.
evals.expected_output: Should route to report id link-recovery, which finds URLs that previously earned search clicks but now fail, redirect poorly, or block indexing. Should note it needs Search Console page history and is not a backlink report. Should follow the discovery flow: seo_list_reports with category monitoring, seo_describe_report with id link-recovery, then seo_run_report with site. Should read range and summary.checked with severity totals first, then per-item original and final URL, GSC metrics, issue lists, and redirect trace, then chain status, final-page indexability, canonicals, and warnings.
assertions: Uses report id link-recovery for the broken search-value URL review
Follows seo_list_reports, seo_describe_report, seo_run_report in order
Notes it needs Search Console page history and is not a backlink report
Reads range and summary.checked with severity totals first
Reads per-item original and final URL, GSC metrics, and redirect trace
Reads chain status, final-page indexability, and canonicals

evals.prompt: Agent task: run link-recovery for sc-domain:example.com over 180 days, top 30, at least 50 clicks in the window.
evals.expected_output: Should run report id link-recovery with site sc-domain:example.com, days 180, limit 30, and minClicks 50, via seo reports run link-recovery or seo_run_report. Should use only parameters defined in the schema. Should note limit constrains coverage, so the queue is a bounded subset rather than every affected URL.
assertions: Runs report id link-recovery with days 180 and limit 30
Sets minClicks 50 as requested
Uses only parameters defined in the link-recovery schema
Notes limit constrains coverage rather than covering every URL
Reads severity totals before the per-item detail

evals.prompt: Total the clicksAtRisk across the queue. That is the traffic we will get back once we redirect all of these.
evals.expected_output: Should refuse. clicksAtRisk and impressionsAtRisk describe the checked period, not a forecast of loss or recovery. Prior visibility orders the queue but does not prove fixing a URL restores traffic. Should recommend confirming the page should still exist and adding one direct 301 to the closest equivalent destination before re-tracing, rather than totalling clicksAtRisk into a recovery figure.
assertions: Refuses to total clicksAtRisk into a recovery figure
States clicksAtRisk and impressionsAtRisk describe the checked period, not a forecast
States prior visibility does not prove fixing a URL restores traffic
Recommends confirming the page should exist before adding a 301
Recommends re-tracing after the redirect to confirm

evals.prompt: The tool could not check some of the URLs, so count those as fine, and while you are at it show me who links to them.
evals.expected_output: Should not count the unchecked URLs as fine and should decline the backlink request. Failed checks or selection limits constrain coverage, so they are not an all-clear. link-recovery is not a backlink report. Should recommend re-running or tracing a single chain with report id redirect-trace rather than treating a failed check as clean.
assertions: Refuses to count failed or unchecked URLs as fine
States failed checks or selection limits constrain coverage
States link-recovery is not a backlink report
Points to report id redirect-trace to trace a single chain
Keeps a failed check distinct from an all-clear
